title: [SYS] Set up Python project structure with pyproject.toml
body:
## Objective
Establish the foundational Python package structure for SVF core.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `pyproject.toml` defined with project metadata, dependencies, and build configuration
- `src/svf/` package layout created
- Package is pip-installable locally via `pip install -e .`
- `tests/` directory scaffolded with a placeholder test

## Implements
SVF-DEV-060, SVF-DEV-065

title: [SYS] Validate fmpy installation with a reference FMU
body:
## Objective
Confirm that fmpy is correctly installed and can load, initialise, and step a known-good FMU.

## Acceptance Criteria
- fmpy added as a dependency in `pyproject.toml`
- A reference FMU (from the fmpy test suite or modelica.org) is included under `examples/`
- A validation script `examples/validate_fmpy.py` successfully loads and steps the FMU for 10 time steps
- Output is printed to stdout showing variable values at each step

## Implements
SVF-DEV-001

title: [SIM] Implement SimulationMaster class with fixed-timestep execution
body:
## Objective
Implement the core `SimulationMaster` class that loads an FMU and steps it at a fixed timestep.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `SimulationMaster` accepts an FMU path and a timestep `dt` as constructor arguments
- Exposes `initialise()`, `step()`, and `teardown()` methods (SVF-DEV-006)
- Runs a configurable number of steps or until a stop time is reached
- Handles FMU initialisation errors gracefully with descriptive messages (SVF-DEV-007)
- Unit tests cover: successful init, step execution, teardown, and init failure handling

## Implements
SVF-DEV-001, SVF-DEV-002, SVF-DEV-006, SVF-DEV-007

title: [SIM] Add variable-timestep execution support to SimulationMaster
body:
## Objective
Extend `SimulationMaster` to support variable-timestep stepping where the FMU exposes step-size negotiation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `SimulationMaster` detects if the loaded FMU supports step-size negotiation
- If supported, uses negotiated step size; otherwise falls back to fixed `dt`
- Behaviour is documented in the class docstring
- Unit test covers variable-step negotiation with a suitable test FMU

## Implements
SVF-DEV-003

title: [SIM] Implement CSV output logger for FMU variables
body:
## Objective
Record all FMU output variables to a timestamped CSV file for each simulation run.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A `CsvLogger` class subscribes to FMU output variables after each step
- Output file is named `{run_id}_{timestamp}.csv` and written to a configurable output directory
- CSV columns: `time`, followed by one column per logged variable
- Logger is attached to `SimulationMaster` via a clean interface (not hardcoded)
- Unit test verifies CSV content against expected values for a known FMU

## Implements
SVF-DEV-005

title: [SIM] Implement SSP-based multi-FMU co-simulation support
body:
## Objective
Extend `SimulationMaster` to load and connect multiple FMUs described by an SSP system description file.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `SimulationMaster` accepts an SSP file path as an alternative to a single FMU path
- Parses SSP to identify component FMUs and their port connections
- Steps all FMUs in the correct dependency order
- Connection values are passed between FMUs at each timestep
- Integration test validates a two-FMU connected system

## Implements
SVF-DEV-004

title: [SYS] Write REQUIREMENTS.md with initial SVF development requirements
body:
## Objective
Establish the baseline requirements document for SVF development.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `REQUIREMENTS.md` committed to repo root
- Covers all functional areas: SIM, BUS, ORC, CAM, MOD, REP, SYS
- Minimum 40 requirements defined
- All M1-scope requirements marked `BASELINED`
- Deferred requirements clearly marked `DEFERRED`
- Traceability index table included at the bottom

## Note
Draft content has been prepared — this issue tracks committing and baselining it.

## Implements
SVF-DEV-031

title: [SYS] Write docs/architecture.md
body:
## Objective
Commit the architecture document to the repository as the canonical reference for SVF design decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `docs/architecture.md` committed and readable via GitHub web interface
- Covers: design principles, layered architecture diagram, all five layer descriptions, technology stack table, model authoring philosophy, real-time roadmap, milestones, and out-of-scope items
- Links from `README.md` to `docs/architecture.md`

## Note
Draft content has been prepared — this issue tracks committing and baselining it.

title: [SYS] Set up GitHub Actions CI pipeline
body:
## Objective
Establish a basic CI pipeline that runs on every push and pull request.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow file at `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Pipeline runs on: push to `main`, push to any `feature/*` branch, pull requests to `main`
- Steps: checkout, install dependencies, run mypy, run pytest, upload JUnit XML results
- Pipeline passes on a clean repo with placeholder tests
- Badge added to `README.md`

## Implements
SVF-DEV-067

title: [SYS] Add type annotations to SimulationMaster and CsvLogger
body:
## Objective
Ensure all public API surface in M1 components is fully type-annotated and passes mypy strict mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `SimulationMaster` and `CsvLogger` have complete type annotations on all public methods and attributes
- `mypy --strict src/svf/` passes with zero errors
- mypy check added to CI pipeline

## Implements
SVF-DEV-068

title: [SYS] Create Dockerfile for simulation execution environment
body:
## Objective
Package the SVF simulation environment in a Docker image so runs are fully reproducible with no host dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `Dockerfile` at repo root builds successfully
- Image is based on a pinned Ubuntu 22.04 base
- SVF core package is installed inside the image
- Running the container with a mounted FMU path executes a simulation and writes CSV output to a mounted output directory
- README includes a minimal `docker run` usage example

## Implements
SVF-DEV-061, SVF-DEV-064
